title: How to Request Access to a New Software Tool or Application
text:
Q: How do I request access to a new software tool or application?
A: To request access to a software tool or application that is not already in the Software Catalog, follow these steps. Step 1: Visit the IT Self-Service Portal (accessible via the company intranet or by navigating to it.company.com/requests). Step 2: Log in with your company SSO credentials. Step 3: Click 'New Request' and select 'Software Access Request' from the request type dropdown. Step 4: Fill in the form — enter the name of the software, the business justification for needing it, and whether a company-wide or individual license is required. Step 5: Specify your manager's name for approval; most software requests require manager sign-off before IT will provision access. Step 6: Submit the form. You will receive an automated email confirming your request number (e.g., SR-1042). Step 7: IT will review the request within 2 business days. If the software requires a new license purchase or security review, this may take up to 5 business days. Step 8: Once approved, you will receive an email with installation instructions or login credentials. If the software is added to the catalog, install it from the Software Catalog using your standard company credentials. Step 9: For urgent requests, contact the IT Helpdesk directly and reference your request number. If your request is denied, the rejection email will include the reason and alternative tools that are already approved.
